scope: CeTTa builtin heads and dedicated tests

entries.head: match
entries.notes: Unknown-space fallback-to-&self bug fixed on 2026-03-25.

entries.head: unify
entries.notes: Good positive coverage plus a dedicated wrong-arity regression.

entries.head: chain
entries.notes: Positive coverage plus a dedicated wrong-arity regression.

entries.head: switch
entries.notes: Positive coverage plus dedicated wrong-arity regressions for both switch heads.

entries.head: case
entries.notes: Positive coverage plus a dedicated wrong-arity regression.

entries.head: collapse-bind
entries.notes: Spec-driven positive coverage now checks both the packed multi-result shape and a ground-atom case, plus a dedicated wrong-arity regression.

entries.head: superpose-bind
entries.notes: Spec-driven positive coverage now checks atom restoration, env threading, residual-equality round-trip, assignment-plus-constraint coexistence, and contradiction rejection. Local HE 0.2.10 still panics on the literal and round-trip restore probes, so the strongest positives remain doc/interpreter-contract-backed rather than CLI-oracle-backed, but CeTTa's Bindings runtime now supports the full documented payload shape.

entries.head: assert
entries.notes: Extensively exercised through HE oracle suites.

entries.head: eval
entries.notes: Positive coverage plus a dedicated wrong-arity regression.

entries.head: cons-atom / decons-atom
entries.notes: Positive and negative coverage now matches the live HE CLI surface.

entries.head: function / return
entries.notes: Return and NoReturn surfaces both exercised.

entries.head: superpose
entries.notes: Positive coverage plus a dedicated wrong-arity regression.

entries.head: context-space
entries.notes: Runtime op was missing and was fixed on 2026-03-25; wrong-arity coverage was added in the audit pass.

entries.head: call-native
entries.notes: CeTTa now explicitly fences direct user-level call-native with the same error surface observed in local HE. Positive native dispatch remains intentionally unsupported from user MeTTa because the docs mark it internal-only.

entries.head: evalc
entries.notes: Positive coverage plus HE-shaped unknown-space regression.

entries.head: metta
entries.notes: Both success and type-error behavior are exercised.

entries.head: new-space / bind!
entries.notes: Widely used positive coverage plus dedicated wrong-arity regressions.

entries.head: add-atom / remove-atom
entries.notes: Unknown-space HE compatibility was fixed on 2026-03-25.

entries.head: get-atoms
entries.notes: Direct positive regression added on 2026-03-25.

entries.head: get-metatype
entries.notes: HE-backed positive coverage plus a dedicated wrong-arity regression.

entries.head: count-atoms
entries.notes: CeTTa-only space-cardinality extension used by overlap benchmarks. It is not present in the local HE docs/stdlib/spec stack, so it should not be counted as part of the HE-compatible core surface.

entries.head: register-module! / import!
entries.notes: Positive path covered plus dedicated import-surface wrong-arity regressions.

entries.head: git-module!
entries.notes: Provider-backed implementation on the typed git-remote seam now matches local HE on the positive local-file fixture, wrong-arity behavior, and TryFetchLatest-style cache refresh for repeated registration of the same file:// repo. The seam carries explicit locator-kind and revision-policy metadata for future remote/catalog providers. Remaining divergence is intentionally documented rather than treated as compatibility debt: CeTTa keeps cleaner CLI-shaped failure text and does not copy HE's broader catalog/environment coupling.

entries.head: with-space-snapshot
entries.notes: Positive coverage plus explicit HE passthrough regressions for wrong arity and non-space inputs.

entries.head: new-state / get-state / change-state!
entries.notes: Positive coverage plus dedicated wrong-arity and first-argument BadArgType regressions.

entries.head: pragma!
entries.notes: CeTTa covers the documented directives (`type-check auto`, `interpreter bare-minimal`, `max-stack-depth` with non-negative validation), wrong-arity and key-type errors, and HE's generic symbol-key setting surface outside bare-minimal. The implementation now routes these through a session-local evaluator-options seam rather than evaluator globals, which is a cleaner base for future dialect/DSL settings. Remaining divergence is intentionally documented rather than treated as compatibility debt: exact host-runtime-specific `StackOverflow` surfacing under low stack limits is an engineering policy question, not core language debt.

entries.head: foldl-atom
entries.notes: CeTTa covers the public HE fold surface directly. Current HE oracle still mis-evaluates the specific `chain (collapse ...) -> foldl-atom` composition; see tests/DISCREPANCIES.md.

entries.head: get-doc
entries.notes: CeTTa covers the HE-facing docs/help lane. The zero-arg function-type repair keeps get-doc/help dispatch single and stable even where the current HE oracle still duplicates or misfires.
